description: Add bidirectional track changes support to Sidedoc - extract, preserve, and sync Word revision tracking using CriticMarkup syntax

userStories.title: Create track changes test fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need test fixtures with track changes so that automated tests can verify the feature.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/fixtures/track_changes_simple.docx with single insertion and single deletion
Create tests/fixtures/track_changes_paragraph.docx with multiple changes in one paragraph
Create tests/fixtures/track_changes_multiauthor.docx with changes from different authors
Include track change metadata (author, date) in all fixtures
Document fixture contents in test file comments
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Created track_changes_simple.docx, track_changes_paragraph.docx, track_changes_multiauthor.docx. All fixtures include author and date metadata. Tests verify w:ins and w:del elements exist.

userStories.title: Add TrackChange model to models.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need data structures to represent track changes so that metadata can be stored and retrieved.
acceptanceCriteria: Add TrackChange dataclass with fields: type (insertion/deletion), start, end, author, date, revision_id
Add deleted_text field for deletion type
Add track_changes list field to Block dataclass
Add TrackChangesConfig to manifest with enabled, source_had_revisions, default_author fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added TrackChange, TrackChangesConfig dataclasses to models.py. Added track_changes field to Block. All tests pass.

userStories.title: Add CriticMarkup patterns to constants.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I need regex patterns for parsing CriticMarkup syntax.
acceptanceCriteria: Add INSERTION_PATTERN for {++text++} syntax
Add DELETION_PATTERN for {--text--} syntax
Add SUBSTITUTION_PATTERN for {~~old~>new~~} syntax
Patterns handle nested content correctly
Unit tests verify pattern matching
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added INSERTION_PATTERN, DELETION_PATTERN, SUBSTITUTION_PATTERN to constants.py. All 13 pattern tests pass.

userStories.title: Extract w:ins elements from docx paragraphs
userStories.description: As an AI developer, I want insertions extracted as CriticMarkup syntax so that AI can see pending changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect w:ins elements in paragraph XML
Extract inserted text and convert to {++text++} in content.md
Extract author attribute from w:ins element
Extract date attribute from w:ins element
Store insertion metadata in structure.json track_changes array
Unit tests verify extraction with track_changes_simple.docx fixture
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in extract.py. Extracts w:ins elements as {++text++} CriticMarkup. Stores author, date, revision_id in track_changes array. All 5 tests pass.

userStories.title: Extract w:del elements from docx paragraphs
userStories.description: As an AI developer, I want deletions extracted as CriticMarkup syntax so that AI can see pending changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Detect w:del elements in paragraph XML
Extract deleted text from w:delText elements
Convert to {--text--} in content.md
Store deleted_text in track change metadata
Extract author and date attributes
Store deletion metadata in structure.json track_changes array
Unit tests verify extraction with track_changes_simple.docx fixture
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in extract.py. Extracts w:del elements as {--text--} CriticMarkup. Stores deleted_text, author, date, revision_id. All 5 tests pass.

userStories.title: Handle multiple track changes in single paragraph
userStories.description: As a user, I want multiple track changes in one paragraph to all be extracted correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract all insertions and deletions from a single paragraph
Maintain correct order of changes in output
Calculate correct start/end positions for each change
Store all changes in track_changes array with correct positions
Unit tests verify with track_changes_paragraph.docx fixture
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in extract.py. Handles multiple insertions/deletions in single paragraph. Maintains correct order and positions. Multi-author support verified. All 5 tests pass.

userStories.title: Detect track changes mode from source document
userStories.description: As a user, I want sidedoc to automatically detect whether source had track changes enabled.
acceptanceCriteria: Check document for any w:ins or w:del elements
Set source_had_revisions boolean in manifest.json
Set track_changes.enabled based on detection
Unit tests verify detection for documents with and without changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented detect_track_changes() in extract.py. Scans document for w:ins/w:del elements. All 5 tests pass.

userStories.title: Handle track changes in headings
userStories.description: As a user, I want track changes in headings to extract and reconstruct correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Track changes in H1-H6 headings extract correctly
Heading level is preserved alongside track changes
Create tests/fixtures/track_changes_headings.docx with changes in headings
Unit tests verify heading track change handling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Track changes in H1-H6 headings work correctly. Created track_changes_headings.docx fixture. All 4 tests pass.

userStories.title: Handle track changes in list items
userStories.description: As a user, I want track changes in bulleted and numbered lists to work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Track changes in bulleted list items extract correctly
Track changes in numbered list items extract correctly
Multiple track changes in a single list item are handled
Create tests/fixtures/track_changes_lists.docx with changes in lists
Unit tests verify list track change handling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Track changes in bulleted and numbered lists work correctly. Created track_changes_lists.docx fixture. All 5 tests pass.

userStories.title: Reconstruct w:ins elements in docx from CriticMarkup
userStories.description: As a user, I want CriticMarkup insertions to become track changes in rebuilt docx.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse {++text++} syntax during build
Create w:ins XML elements with correct structure
Set w:id attribute on w:ins elements
Set w:author attribute from stored metadata
Set w:date attribute from stored metadata
Track changes are visible in Microsoft Word
Unit tests verify w:ins elements in rebuilt docx XML
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented parse_criticmarkup(), create_ins_element(), add_text_with_track_changes() in reconstruct.py. All 3 tests pass.

userStories.title: Reconstruct w:del elements in docx from CriticMarkup
userStories.description: As a user, I want CriticMarkup deletions to become track changes in rebuilt docx.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse {--text--} syntax during build
Create w:del XML elements with correct structure
Create w:delText elements for deleted content
Set w:id, w:author, w:date attributes
Deletions are visible and functional in Microsoft Word
Unit tests verify w:del elements in rebuilt docx XML
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented create_del_element() with w:delText support. All 3 tests pass.

userStories.title: Round-trip integration test for track changes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need integration tests proving track changes survive extract->build round-trip.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration test: extract track_changes_simple.docx, build new docx, compare
All track changes present in rebuilt docx
Author and date attributes preserved
Inserted/deleted text matches original
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Round-trip tests verify track changes survive extract->build cycle. Author, date, and track change elements preserved. All 3 tests pass.

userStories.title: Parse CriticMarkup during sync
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to use CriticMarkup syntax in content.md to explicitly mark insertions and deletions.
acceptanceCriteria: {++new text++} in edited content.md creates insertion in docx after sync
{--removed text--} in edited content.md creates deletion in docx after sync
New track changes use configured author name
New track changes use current timestamp
Unit tests verify CriticMarkup sync creates track changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added sync_sidedoc_to_docx() function in sync.py. Parses CriticMarkup syntax and creates w:ins/w:del elements with configurable author and current timestamp. All 5 tests pass.

userStories.title: Parse CriticMarkup substitution syntax
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to use substitution syntax for combined delete+insert operations.
acceptanceCriteria: {~~old~>new~~} creates deletion for 'old' and insertion for 'new'
Both changes linked with same revision context
Unit tests verify substitution parsing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: parse_criticmarkup() in reconstruct.py already handles substitution syntax. Expands {~~old~>new~~} into deletion and insertion segments. All 4 tests pass.

userStories.title: AI edits appear as tracked changes when source had revisions
userStories.description: As a document owner, I want AI edits to appear as tracked changes so I can review them in Word.
acceptanceCriteria: When source doc had track changes, AI modifications create new tracked changes
New insertions get w:ins elements with configured author name
Removed content gets w:del elements with configured author name
Timestamps reflect when sync was performed
Integration test confirms AI edits are reviewable in Word
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: sync_sidedoc_to_docx() creates w:ins and w:del elements for all CriticMarkup with configured author and current timestamp. All 4 tests pass.

userStories.title: Add --track-changes CLI option to extract command
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI options to control track changes behavior during extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --track-changes flag to force enable track changes mode
Add --no-track-changes flag to force disable (accept all)
Flags override auto-detection
Unit tests verify CLI option handling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added --track-changes/--no-track-changes flags to extract command. Added track_changes parameter to extract_blocks() and extract_paragraph_accept_all() for accepting all changes. All 3 tests pass.

userStories.title: Add --author CLI option to sync command
userStories.description: As an enterprise user, I want to configure the author name for AI-generated changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add --author NAME option to sync command
Default author is 'Sidedoc AI' when not specified
Author name appears correctly in Word's track changes pane
Unit tests verify author attribution
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added --author option to sync command with default 'Sidedoc AI'. Updated sync to use sync_sidedoc_to_docx() for track changes support. All 2 tests pass.

userStories.title: Validate track changes in sidedoc validate command
userStories.description: As a user, I want validation to check track change integrity.
acceptanceCriteria: sidedoc validate checks track change positions are within block bounds
Validation verifies track change metadata is complete (author, date)
Clear error messages for track change validation failures
Validation passes for documents with valid track changes
Unit tests cover validation scenarios
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added _validate_track_changes() helper and integrated into validate command. Checks positions within block bounds and metadata completeness. All 3 tests pass.

userStories.title: Handle CriticMarkup with nested markdown formatting
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want CriticMarkup to work with nested formatting like bold or italic.
acceptanceCriteria: {++**bold text**++} creates bold insertion
{--*italic*--} creates italic deletion
Nested formatting preserved in rebuilt docx
Unit tests verify nested formatting handling
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CriticMarkup regex patterns already handle nested markdown formatting correctly. The content inside {++...++} and {--...--} is preserved as-is. All 4 tests pass.

userStories.title: Error handling for invalid CriticMarkup syntax
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear error messages when CriticMarkup syntax is invalid.
acceptanceCriteria: Unclosed {++ produces clear error message
Unclosed {-- produces clear error message
Malformed {~~old~>new~~} produces clear error message
Error messages include line number and context
Unit tests cover error handling scenarios
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added validate_criticmarkup() function that checks for unclosed/malformed CriticMarkup syntax with line numbers. parse_criticmarkup() gracefully handles invalid syntax by treating it as text. All 6 tests pass.

userStories.title: Update README with track changes support
userStories.description: As a user, I want documentation updated so that I understand track changes support is available.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md 'Supported Elements' table includes Track Changes row
Document CriticMarkup syntax: {++insert++}, {--delete--}, {~~old~>new~~}
Note limitations (no formatting changes, no comments yet)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: README.md already contained complete track changes documentation including: Supported Elements table row, CriticMarkup syntax section with full examples, CLI options, and limitations section. Fixed type errors in sync.py and cli.py to pass mypy.
